title: Cloud Run request lifecycle, CPU allocation, and concurrency
description: Explains per-instance concurrency, CPU allocated only during requests vs always-on, autoscaling on concurrency, and the container contract.
body: Use when reasoning about Cloud Run behavior. Do not assume background goroutines keep running between requests by default. Actions: understand that an instance serves up to its concurrency setting in parallel, CPU is throttled outside request handling unless CPU-always-allocated is set, autoscaling reacts to concurrent requests and queue depth, and the container must listen on $PORT and start fast. Inspect request logs and instance count. Validation signals include instance count tracking load and background work completing only when CPU-always-on is enabled. Risk boundary: enabling always-on CPU raises cost and should be a deliberate choice.
